 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8","title":"Rollout track: governance, security/privacy, migration, and operations docs","description":"## Track objective
Operationalize the feature safely: feature flags, migration/rollback, security/privacy controls, runbooks, and user/operator documentation.

## Why this track exists
A high-throughput recorder can become a high-risk subsystem without explicit governance on retention, privacy, cost, and rollback.

## Deliverables
- Phased rollout plan with canary and kill-switch controls
- Security/privacy model (redaction, access boundaries, retention)
- Migration and rollback playbooks
- End-user and operator docs with troubleshooting workflows

## Acceptance criteria
- Production rollout can be paused/reverted without data ambiguity.
- Ops docs are sufficient for independent on-call execution.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.770578Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:17.118202Z","labels":["docs","ops","privacy","rollout","security"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.770578Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:17.077136Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:17.118153Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","title":"Define staged feature-flag rollout plan with explicit go/no-go/rollback criteria","description":"## Context
Recorder/search rollout must be staged behind feature flags with clear go/no-go criteria to reduce operational risk.

## Scope
Define phased rollout plan (off -> shadow -> limited -> default-on) with explicit guardrails and rollback triggers.

## Deliverables
- Feature flags and config defaults per phase
- Exit criteria and rollback triggers for each phase
- Stakeholder communication checklist for phase changes
- Decision log template for rollout approvals

## Why this matters
Controlled rollout avoids destabilizing core ft workflows while introducing deep new infrastructure.

## Acceptance
- Every phase has measurable entry/exit criteria
- Rollback can be executed quickly and safely
- Flags are documented and testable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.501988Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.736785Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","governance","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.501988Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.736721Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","title":"Plan and validate migration path from existing capture/search to recorder architecture","description":"## Context
Existing users/workflows need a safe migration path from current capture/search behavior to the new recorder architecture.

## Scope
Create migration strategy covering data transition, compatibility mode, and cutover sequencing.

## Deliverables
- Migration phases (parallel run, validation, cutover, fallback)
- Data compatibility requirements between old and new paths
- Tooling/checklists for migration verification
- Backout plan preserving existing functionality

## Why this matters
A technically strong system still fails if migration is risky or opaque.

## Acceptance
- Migration steps are reproducible and reversible
- Compatibility assumptions are explicit and tested
- Operators have clear success/failure indicators during cutover","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.521204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.770614Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","migration","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.521204Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.626822Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.753758Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.6.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.770568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","title":"Define governance policy for retention, privacy, redaction, and privileged access","description":"## Context
Full transcript capture can include sensitive content. Governance policy (retention, access, redaction classes, audit expectations) must be explicit before wide enablement.

## Scope
Define security/privacy governance package for recorder data lifecycle and access.

## Deliverables
- Policy doc for retention classes, redaction taxonomy, and access tiers
- Compliance and data-handling rationale for stored transcripts
- Approval workflow for privileged access and exceptions
- Policy-to-implementation traceability map

## Why this matters
Governance is a hard prerequisite for responsible always-on logging.

## Acceptance
- Policies are concrete enough to implement and test
- Ownership/accountability for policy decisions is explicit
- Engineering controls are traceable to governance requirements","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.54189Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.527624Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","policy","privacy","security","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.54189Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.787277Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","title":"Create ops runbooks and alert strategy for recorder/index/search reliability","description":"## Context
Operations teams need practical runbooks for health monitoring, degradation handling, and incident escalation.

## Scope
Produce operational playbooks and alert strategy for recorder/index/search pipeline.

## Deliverables
- Runbook covering healthy-state checks and common failure signatures
- Alert definitions/severity mapping for lag/errors/corruption risks
- Escalation and ownership matrix
- Maintenance procedures (reindex, retention actions, failover)

## Why this matters
Operational clarity is required for confident long-term ownership.

## Acceptance
- Runbook enables on-call response without deep implementation context
- Alerts map clearly to actionable remediation steps
- Ownership and escalation paths are explicit","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.56325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.838132Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.56325Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.646322Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.82019Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.7.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.838082Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","title":"Define recorder-specific incident response and postmortem process","description":"## Context
Incidents in a recorder system are high-stakes due to forensic implications. Response and postmortem structure must be pre-defined.

## Scope
Define incident response and postmortem templates specific to recorder correctness, durability, and privacy failures.

## Deliverables
- Incident classification matrix (data loss, ordering drift, privacy leak, performance collapse)
- Standard response timeline/checklist
- Postmortem template with recorder-specific evidence requirements
- Feedback loop from incidents into bead backlog updates

## Why this matters
Structured incident handling shortens MTTR and preserves trust in recorder evidence.

## Acceptance
- Team can run a tabletop exercise using provided templates
- Evidence requirements are clear and feasible
- Postmortem outputs map to actionable follow-up work","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.586906Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.663396Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","incident-response","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.586906Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.663351Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","title":"Produce adoption/onboarding/handoff package for long-term recorder ownership","description":"## Context
Sustained value requires explicit adoption and handoff: how contributors, operators, and agents use the system day-to-day.

## Scope
Create onboarding/handoff package that translates architecture into practical usage and maintenance rituals.

## Deliverables
- Role-specific onboarding guides (developer/operator/agent)
- Maintenance cadence recommendations (index health, retention review, quality checks)
- Knowledge-transfer checklist for future sessions/owners
- Success metrics for adoption (usage, query efficacy, incident outcomes)

## Why this matters
A self-documenting system only works if onboarding and handoff are engineered, not assumed.

## Acceptance
- New contributor can execute core workflows from docs alone
- Handoff checklist supports continuity across sessions
- Adoption metrics are defined and measurable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.607138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.717487Z","labels":["docs","epic","flight-recorder","handoff","track-rollout"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.607138Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.682046Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.699663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.8.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:08:01.717443Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
@@ -1125,7 +1125,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-rfxu","title":"Perf budgets + regression checks (ingest tick, pattern match, FTS inserts)","description":"# Task: Perf budgets + regression checks

## Goal
Define and enforce basic performance budgets so wa stays fast as features accrete.

## Key hot paths
- Quick reject / prefiltering (if applicable)
- Delta extraction + seq assignment
- Pattern detection over deltas
- DB writes (segments + FTS)

## Deliverables
- Criterion benches for hot paths (some may already exist; extend to cover end-to-end ingest tick).
- Budget thresholds (warn or fail) suitable for CI.

## Testing / CI expectations
- Use coarse budgets to avoid noisy failures.
- CI should detect gross regressions (order-of-magnitude) reliably and upload benchmark artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI can detect a gross regression (e.g., 10× slowdown) reliably.
- Benches are stable (avoid noisy wall-clock assertions; use coarse budgets).
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:27:14.595077934Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.186609-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:11:15.589881476Z"}
 {"id":"wa-rioy","title":"E2E: backpressure stress scenario (graceful degradation + artifacts)","description":"# Task: E2E backpressure stress scenario

## Goal
Prove backpressure behavior in an end-to-end run.

## Scenario
- Generate high-output workload (prefer deterministic fixture playback).
- Verify:
  - watcher remains responsive
  - health surfaces show lag
  - GAP emitted when continuity cannot be guaranteed

## Requirements
- No fixed sleeps; use quiescence.
- Artifacts:
  - health snapshots over time
  - logs
  - DB slice of gaps/events

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E demonstrates bounded behavior and debuggability.

 {"id":"wa-rjia","title":"TUI health metrics panel: queue depth, lag, DB stats, circuit breakers","description":"
# TUI Health Metrics Panel

## Purpose
Display system health at a glance to help users understand if wa is operating normally.

## UI Design
```
├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Health: ● OK  │ Queue: 0  │ Lag: 12ms  │ DB: 1.2GB                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Metrics to Display
1. **Overall Status**: OK / Warning / Error (aggregated)
2. **Queue Depth**: Write queue size (warning if > 100)
3. **Ingest Lag**: Time since last poll (warning if > 1s)
4. **DB Size**: Current database size
5. **Circuit Breakers**: Any tripped breakers highlighted
6. **Memory**: Current memory usage (optional)

## Color Coding
- Green (●): All systems healthy
- Yellow (◐): Some warnings, not critical
- Red (◉): Critical issues need attention

## Implementation
- Poll health_snapshot from watcher runtime
- Update every 1 second
- Aggregate individual metrics into overall status
- Expandable to show detailed metrics

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Health panel shows aggregated status
- [ ] Individual metrics visible at a glance
- [ ] Color coding reflects severity
- [ ] Click/expand shows detailed breakdown

## Testing
- Manual smoke test: metrics panel renders queue depth and DB stats.
- Verify refresh loop does not block TUI input.

 {"id":"wa-rnf","title":"[EPIC] CLI Polish: Shell Completions, Aliases, Progressive Disclosure","description":"# [EPIC] CLI Polish

## Mission
Make the wa CLI feel **polished and professional**—the kind of tool where attention to detail builds trust.

## Why This Matters
CLI UX is cumulative. Many small improvements add up to a tool that feels delightful:
- Tab completion → fewer typos, faster navigation
- Smart aliases → muscle memory efficiency
- Progressive disclosure → clean by default, detailed on request
- Consistent patterns → predictable behavior

## Components

### 1. Shell Completions
Full tab completion for all commands:
```bash
$ wa <TAB>
watch    status   events   send     workflow   ...

$ wa send --pane <TAB>
1 (codex @ /home/user/project)
3 (claude @ /home/user/other)

$ wa workflow run <TAB>
handle_compaction    handle_usage_limits    handle_auth_required
```

Implementation:
- clap derives completions for bash/zsh/fish/powershell
- Dynamic completions for pane IDs, workflow names
- `wa completions bash > ~/.bash_completion.d/wa`

### 2. Smart Aliases
Built-in short forms for common operations:
```bash
was     → wa status
wae     → wa events --unhandled
waq     → wa query
waw     → wa watch
wat     → wa timeline
```

Also support user-defined aliases in config.

### 3. Progressive Disclosure
Output verbosity levels:
- Default: Essential info only
- `-v`: Additional context
- `-vv`: Debug details
- `--format json`: Machine-readable (stable schema; intended for scripts/automation)

Notes:
- `-v/-vv` controls *how much* information we include.
- `--format {auto|plain|json}` controls *how* we render it (TTY-rich vs stable plain vs machine JSON).

Example:
```bash
$ wa status
Panes: 4 observed, 1 ignored
Events: 2 unhandled

$ wa status -v
Panes:
  1: codex [PromptActive] /home/user/project (last: 2m ago)
  3: claude [CommandRunning] /home/user/other (last: 5s ago)
  ...

Events (unhandled):
  - codex.usage_limit_reached (Pane 1, 2m ago)
  - session.compaction (Pane 3, 5m ago)
```

### 4. Consistent Help Patterns
Every command has:
- `--help`: Usage and options
- `-h`: Brief usage
- Examples in help text
- Cross-references to related commands

```bash
$ wa send --help
wa send - Send text to a pane

USAGE:
    wa send --pane <ID> [OPTIONS] <TEXT>

OPTIONS:
    --pane <ID>      Target pane ID (required)
    --dry-run        Preview without executing
    --wait-for <PAT> Wait for pattern after send
    --timeout <SEC>  Wait timeout (default: 30)

EXAMPLES:
    wa send --pane 3 "hello"
    wa send --pane 3 --dry-run "hello"
    wa send --pane 3 --wait-for ">>>" "continue"

SEE ALSO:
    wa status       List available panes
    wa why          Explain send failures
```

### 5. Output Consistency
All commands follow the same patterns:
- Success: Clean output, exit 0
- Error: Error message with remediation, exit 1
- JSON mode: Structured output with envelope
- No ANSI when piped

## Testing
- Completion tests: Verify completions are generated correctly
- Alias tests: Aliases resolve to correct commands
- Output tests: Progressive disclosure works as expected
- Help tests: All commands have valid help text

## Success Criteria
- Tab completion works for all commands and dynamic values
- Aliases are documented and work as expected
- Output verbosity is consistent across all commands
- Help text is comprehensive and includes examples

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shell completions are generated and kept in sync with CLI.
- Aliases and verbosity levels behave consistently.
- Help output follows a unified style across commands.
- wa-rnf.5 tests pass.


## Testing
- Unit: clap completion generation + alias expansion + verbosity routing.
- Snapshot/contract: help output + non-TTY no-ANSI checks; JSON output schema validation.
- CI: smoke tests ensure generated completions and help text remain in sync.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:44:27.386061275Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:43:38.779353919Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:43:38.779195575Z"}
 {"id":"wa-rnf.1","title":"Shell completions: static clap derivation + dynamic pane/workflow completion","description":"
# Shell Completions

## Purpose
Enable tab completion for all wa commands, including dynamic values like pane IDs and workflow names.

## Static Completions (clap derive)
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(name = "wa")]
struct Cli {
    #[command(subcommand)]
    command: Commands,
}

// In main.rs
if let Some(shell) = args.completions {
    clap_complete::generate(shell, &mut cli, "wa", &mut io::stdout());
}
```

## Dynamic Completions
For pane IDs:
```bash
$ wa send --pane <TAB>
1 (codex @ /home/user/project)
3 (claude @ /home/user/other)
```

For workflow names:
```bash
$ wa workflow run <TAB>
handle_compaction    handle_usage_limits
```

## Implementation
Use clap_complete's custom completer:
```rust
fn complete_pane_id(current: &str) -> Vec<CompletionCandidate> {
    // Query running wa instance for pane list
    // Format as completion candidates
}
```

## Installation
```bash
# Bash
wa completions bash > ~/.bash_completion.d/wa

# Zsh
wa completions zsh > ~/.zfunc/_wa

# Fish
wa completions fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/wa.fish
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Static completions for all commands
- [ ] Dynamic completion for pane IDs
- [ ] Dynamic completion for workflow names
- [ ] Installation instructions in setup wizard
- [ ] Works in bash, zsh, fish

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.

 {"id":"wa-rnf.2","title":"Smart command aliases: built-in short forms + user-configurable aliases","description":"
# Smart Command Aliases

## Purpose
Provide muscle-memory-friendly shortcuts for common operations.

## Built-in Aliases
```
was  → wa status
wae  → wa events --unhandled
waq  → wa query
waw  → wa watch
wat  → wa timeline
wad  → wa doctor
```

## Implementation
In wa.toml:
```toml
[aliases]
s = "status"
e = "events --unhandled"
q = "query"
t = "timeline"
d = "doctor"
```

In code:
```rust
fn resolve_alias(args: &[String], config: &Config) -> Vec<String> {
    if let Some(alias) = config.aliases.get(&args[0]) {
        let expanded = shell_words::split(alias)?;
        [expanded, args[1..].to_vec()].concat()
    } else {
        args.to_vec()
    }
}
```

## Shell Aliases (Optional)
```bash
# Add to .bashrc
alias was='wa status'
alias wae='wa events --unhandled'
```

Generated by: `wa setup aliases bash >> ~/.bashrc`

## Listing Aliases
```
$ wa aliases
Built-in:
  s  → status
  e  → events --unhandled
  ...

User-defined:
  x  → export --format=jsonl
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Built-in aliases work
- [ ] User aliases configurable in wa.toml
- [ ] `wa aliases` lists all aliases
- [ ] Shell alias generation helper
- [ ] No conflicts with subcommand names

## Testing
- Unit tests for completion/alias generation and help formatting.
- Integration tests for help output snapshots and no-ANSI guarantees.
- CI smoke tests for completions and help stability.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:55:11.174016785Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T05:22:16.04006002Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T05:22:16.039909741Z"}
